vibe: Late-night Tokyo drift — deep house grooves, city pop warmth, lo-fi soul, Latin heat, Melbourne funk, UK jazz, math rock precision, and dub techno fog

tracks.name: Do it Again
tracks.reason: You've been deep in Soichi Terada's world — Yokota is his Tokyo deep house soulmate. Same warm, rolling grooves but with a cheekier dancefloor energy.

tracks.name: BRASILIAN SKIES
tracks.reason: With Momoko Kikuchi and 加藤有紀 on repeat, this is the next step — Takanaka is a Japanese funk guitar god who blends tropicalia with fusion. Pure sunshine.

tracks.name: Soul Of Freedom feat. Cise Starr
tracks.reason: You've got Nujabes' City Lights on heavy rotation — Uyama Hiroto was his closest collaborator and this track carries that same spiritual jazz-hop DNA.

tracks.name: Vámono
tracks.reason: You've been listening to Tainy — Buscabulla is Puerto Rican electronic duo that takes reggaeton's pulse and wraps it in dreamy synth-pop. Way more textured than the mainstream.

tracks.name: The Early Bird Catches
tracks.reason: Since Surprise Chef's Daylight Savings is already in your top tracks, meet their Melbourne funk neighbours. Same scene, same heat — instrumental soul that swaggers.

tracks.name: Run Outs
tracks.reason: You're into Kokoroko's Abusey Junction — Alfa Mist is from the same London jazz scene but leans more into hip-hop influenced keys and broken beat. Moody and gorgeous.

tracks.name: グッドバイ -album version-
tracks.reason: Japanese math rock at its most emotional — intricate guitar interplay that builds to something genuinely moving. If you like precision and feeling at the same time, this is it.

tracks.name: Invisible
tracks.reason: With Basic Channel, Deepchord, and Fluxion in your recent plays, Monolake (Robert Henke, co-creator of Ableton Live) is the missing piece of that dub techno puzzle.

tracks.name: Colibria
tracks.reason: Ecuadorian electronic producer who blends Andean folk with deep house — bridges your love of organic grooves and electronic production in a way that feels totally fresh.

tracks.name: 4:00A.M.
tracks.reason: A city pop essential you might've missed — Taeko Onuki's late-night groove is silkier and more melancholic than Tatsuro or Mariya. Perfect for the Tokyo midnight hour.
